description: Trigger discrimination for the compliance cluster: the SoA document vs the management systems that mandate it, the control catalog, the crosswalk, and SOC 2 scoping.

cases.prompt: Draft the per-control applicability document for our ISO 27001 certification — which Annex A controls apply, why, and which we exclude.
cases.reason: Per-control inclusion/exclusion justification tied to risk treatment IS the SoA; the ISMS architect mandates and consumes it, the foundation supplies mechanisms.

cases.prompt: Design the whole ISO 27001 management system for us — scope, leadership, risk process, internal audit, management review.
cases.reason: Clauses 4–10 machinery is the projection; the SoA author writes one (mandatory) artifact inside that system.

cases.prompt: Build our ISO 42001 AI management system, including the AI risk and impact assessment processes.
cases.reason: AIMS clauses 4–10 with 6.1.2/6.1.4 machinery is the 42001 projection; the SoA author only writes the applicability document it mandates.

cases.prompt: Write the underlying control set — objectives, owners, mechanisms — that our compliance work will draw from.
cases.reason: The framework-agnostic catalog is the foundation; the SoA maps Annex A applicability onto those mechanisms.

cases.prompt: Map each of our controls to its ISO 27001 Annex A reference, SOC 2 criterion, and ISO 42001 objective.
cases.reason: Multi-framework reference mapping is the crosswalk; the SoA is a single-standard applicability judgment document, not a reference table.

cases.prompt: Scope our SOC 2 — Security plus which optional categories, and Type 1 or Type 2?
cases.reason: SOC 2 is an attestation with no SoA; category and Type/window scoping belongs to the SOC 2 mapper.
